title: psyllium arenarium subsp. orientale
intro: Psyllium arenarium subsp. orientale, also known as wild psyllium, is a plant species belonging to the Plantaginaceae family, characterized by its fibrous seeds and medicinal properties. The seeds of this plant contain mucilage, a gel-like substance that helps to soothe digestive issues and promote healthy bowel movements, making it a popular natural remedy for constipation and irritable bowel syndrome. The medicinal properties of Psyllium arenarium subsp. orientale include its ability to lower cholesterol levels, regulate blood sugar levels, and support weight management, leading to overall improved health and well-being. In herbal preparations, the seeds of this plant can be used to make capsules, powders, or teas, which can be consumed to reap its medicinal benefits.
